requirements.full_sentence:
If not managed effectively, credit risk may have ramifications for a range of other risk
categories too.
requirements.object: range of other risk categories too

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -
Credit Risk Management) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Risk Management) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
requirements.object: upon issuance

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to commercial banks (hereinafter collectively
referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
requirements.object: banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of these Directions, 'Commercial Banks' shall mean banking
companies (other than Small Finance Banks, Payment Banks, and Local Area
Banks), corresponding new banks, and the State Bank of India, as defined
respectively under clauses (c), (da), and (nc) of Section 5 of the Banking
<image>
Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: than Small Finance Banks, Payment Banks, and Local Area Banks), corresponding new banks, and the State Bank of India, as

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires,
Explanation: Exposure shall mean all fund-based and non-fund-based exposures.
requirements.object: non-fund-based exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
3[(viia) 'Entity' in the context of a 'related party' prescribed in Chapter V shall mean a
'person' other than an individual and a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF).]
Note: For the purpose of assessing the effectiveness of hedge, guidance may be
taken from the applicable accounting standards and the relevant guidance notes of
the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India on the matter.
requirements.object: than an individual and a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF)

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: An exposure shall be considered as naturally hedged only if the
offsetting exposure has the maturity / cash flow within the same accounting year.
requirements.object: hedged only if the offsetting exposure has the maturity / cash flow within the same accounting year

requirements.full_sentence:
For instance, export revenues (booked as receivable) may offset the exchange risk
arising out of repayment obligations of an external commercial borrowing if both
the exposures have cash flows / maturity within the same accounting year.
requirements.object: arising out of repayment obligations of an external commercial borrowing if both the exposures have cash flows / maturity wit

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) 'Bank Guarantee' shall mean financial and performance guarantees issued by
banks on behalf of their clients.
requirements.object: guarantees issued by banks on behalf of their clients

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) 'Bills Purchased and Discounted' shall mean negotiable instruments that give the
holder the right to receive stated fixed sums on demand or at a fixed or determinable
future time.
requirements.object: give the holder the right to receive stated fixed sums on demand or at a fixed or determinable future time

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) 'Cash credit (CC)' shall mean a facility, under which a customer is allowed an
advance up to the credit limit against the security by way of hypothecation / pledge
of goods, book debts, standing crops, etc.
requirements.object: which a customer is allowed an advance up to the credit limit against the security by way of hypothecation / pledge of goods, b

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
1[(iiia) 'Committee on lending to related parties' shall mean a committee of the Board of
the bank entrusted with sanctioning of loans to related parties.
requirements.object: the Board of the bank entrusted with sanctioning of loans to related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks may also
identify any existing Committee, other than the Audit Committee, for this purpose.
requirements.object: Committee, other than the Audit Committee, for this purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
(iiib) 'Contract or arrangement' shall have the same meaning as specified in Section
188(1)(a) to (g) of the Companies Act, 2013.
requirements.object: as specified in Section 188(1)(a) to (g) of the Companies Act, 2013

requirements.full_sentence:
(iiic) 'Control' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under Section 2(27) of the
Companies Act, 2013.]
requirements.object: as assigned to it under Section 2(27) of the Companies Act, 2013

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) 'Current Account' shall mean a form of demand deposit account wherefrom
withdrawals are allowed any number of times depending upon the balance in the
account or up to a particular agreed amount and shall also be deemed to include
other deposit accounts which are neither Savings nor Term deposit account.
requirements.object: demand deposit account wherefrom withdrawals are allowed any number of times depending upon the balance in the account or up to a parti

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) 'Demand loans' shall mean all loans repayable on demand (such as cash credit,
overdraft, bills purchased and discounted, etc.) and short-term loans with maturity
up to one year, whether secured or unsecured, are considered demand loans.
requirements.object: on demand (such as cash credit, overdraft, bills purchased and discounted, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
2[(va) 'Director of a bank' shall have the same meaning as defined in Explanation (b) to
Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act 1949 and would include a nominee
director and an independent director.]
requirements.object: as defined in Explanation (b) to Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act 1949 and would include a nominee director and an indep

requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) 'Earnings before Interest and Depreciation (EBID)' shall have the same meaning as
defined for computation of Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR), i.e., EBID = Profit
After Tax + Depreciation + Interest on debt + Lease Rentals, if any.
requirements.object: as defined for computation of Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR), i.e., EBID = Profit After Tax + Depreciation + Interest on debt + Lease Rentals

requirements.full_sentence:
(vii) 'Entity' in the context of 'Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure' prescribed in
Chapter-VII of these Directions shall mean a counterparty to which a bank has
exposure in any currency.
requirements.object: which a bank has exposure in any currency

requirements.full_sentence:
(xiv) 6['Personal Loan' shall have the same meaning as defined under Banking Statistics
(Harmonised Definitions).]
7[(xiva) 'Promoter' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under Section 2(69) of
the Companies Act, 2013.
requirements.object: as defined under Banking Statistics (Harmonised Definitions)

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(xivb) 'Reciprocally Related Person' means an individual who is either (i) a director
(excluding independent director / Nominee director appointed by the Government
or RBI or a statutory body) of another commercial bank, or an AIFI, or a scheduled
cooperative bank, or a subsidiary of a commercial bank;
requirements.object: either (i) a director (excluding independent director / Nominee director appointed by the Government or RBI or a statutory body)

requirements.full_sentence:
(xivc) 'Related Party' with respect to a bank shall mean a related person, a reciprocally
related person, or any of the following entities:
requirements.object: a reciprocally related person, or any of the following entities:

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Provided that, nothing in sub-clause (e) above shall apply in cases where the authority to
nominate a director arises exclusively from a lending or financing arrangement.
requirements.object: the authority to nominate a director arises exclusively from a lending or financing arrangement

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that, nothing in sub-clause (f) above shall apply to the advice, directions
or instructions given in a professional capacity.
requirements.object: directions or instructions given in a professional capacity

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that, Government of India / State Government-owned or controlled
entities shall not be treated as related parties to a government-owned bank just by virtue
of the fact that the Government has the common ownership or control of such entities.
requirements.object: parties to a government-owned bank just by virtue of the fact that the Government has the common ownership or control of such en

requirements.full_sentence:
(xivd) 'Related Person' with respect to a bank shall mean a person, and the relatives of
such a person, where the person:
requirements.object: the relatives of such a person

requirements.full_sentence:
(xv) 8['Relative' with regard to a natural person shall have the same meaning as defined
in Section 2(77) of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules framed therein.]
requirements.object: as defined in Section 2(77) of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules framed therein

requirements.full_sentence:
(xvi) 'Revaluation Reserve' shall mean a reserve created on the revaluation of assets
or net assets represented by the surplus of the estimated replacement cost or
estimated market values over the book values thereof.
requirements.object: on the revaluation of assets or net assets represented by the surplus of the estimated replacement cost or estimated market val

requirements.full_sentence:
(xvii) 'Smaller entities' in the context of 'Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure'
prescribed in Chapter-VII of these Directions shall mean those entities on which total
exposure of the banking system is at ₹50 crore or less.
requirements.object: which total exposure of the banking system is at ₹50 crore or less

requirements.full_sentence:
(xviii) 'Substantial interest' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section
5(ne) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: as assigned to it in Section 5(ne) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

requirements.full_sentence:
(xix) 'Term loans' shall mean loans which have a specified maturity and is payable in
instalments or in bullet form.
requirements.object: a specified maturity and is payable in instalments or in bullet form

requirements.full_sentence:
(xx) 'Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE)' shall mean Foreign Currency
Exposure (FCE) excluding items which are effective hedge of each other.
requirements.object: (FCE) excluding items which are effective hedge of each other

requirements.full_sentence:
While
estimating UFCE of an entity, banks shall consider only two types of hedges -
financial hedge and natural hedge.
requirements.object: of hedges - financial hedge and natural hedge

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank
of India Act, 1934, and rules / regulations made thereunder, or any statutory
modification or re-enactment thereto or in other relevant regulations issued by the
Reserve Bank, or Glossary of Terms published by Reserve Bank or as used in
commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and rules / regulat

requirements.full_sentence:
10[A bank shall put in place a comprehensive Board approved policy on Credit Risk
Management.
requirements.object: comprehensive Board approved policy on Credit Risk Management

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall, inter alia, cover aspects related to lending to related
parties, country risk management, unhedged foreign currency exposures, valuation
of properties including empanelment of valuers, and opening of current accounts and
CC / OD accounts.
requirements.object: aspects related to lending to related parties, country risk management, unhedged foreign currency exposures, valuation of properti

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall strictly follow the credit risk management guidelines contained in the
'Guidance Note on Credit Risk Management'.
requirements.object: risk management guidelines contained in the 'Guidance Note on Credit Risk Management'

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall carry out their independent and objective credit appraisal in all cases and
must not depend on credit appraisal reports prepared by outside consultants,
especially the in-house consultants of the borrowing entity.
requirements.object: and objective credit appraisal in all cases and must not depend on credit appraisal reports prepared by outside consultant

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank / lender shall carry out sensitivity tests / scenario analysis, especially for
infrastructure projects, which shall inter alia include project delays and cost overruns.
requirements.object: / scenario analysis, especially for infrastructure projects, which shall inter alia include project delays and cost overru

requirements.full_sentence:
This shall aid in taking a view on viability of the project at the time of deciding on
deferment of Date of Commencement of Commercial Operations (DCCO) /
restructuring.
requirements.object: view on viability of the project at the time of deciding on deferment of Date of Commencement of Commercial Operations (DCCO) / restru

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ascertain the source and quality of equity capital brought in by the
promoters / shareholders.
requirements.object: quality of equity capital brought in by the promoters / shareholders

requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, a lender shall
ensure at the time of credit appraisal that debt of the parent company is not infused as
equity capital of the subsidiary / Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV).
requirements.object: of credit appraisal that debt of the parent company is not infused as equity capital of the subsidiary / Special Purpose Vehicle (S

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall review their extant sectoral exposure limits for consumer credit and put
in place, if not already there, Board approved limits in respect of various sub-segments
under consumer credit as may be considered necessary by the Boards as part of
prudent risk management.
requirements.object: exposure limits for consumer credit and put in place

requirements.full_sentence:
In particular, limits shall be prescribed for all unsecured
consumer credit exposures.
requirements.object: unsecured consumer credit exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
The limits so fixed shall be strictly adhered to and
monitored on an ongoing basis by the Risk Management Committee.
requirements.object: and monitored on an ongoing basis by the Risk Management Committee

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall include in their loan policies / credit appraisal processes, suitable
provisions for obtaining Credit Information Reports (CIRs) from one or more Credit
Information Companies (CICs) so that the credit decisions are based on credit
information available in the system.
requirements.object: policies / credit appraisal processes, suitable provisions for obtaining Credit Information Reports (CIRs) from one or more Cred

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall be guided by DoS.CO.PPG.BC.1/11.01.005/2020-21 dated August 21,
2020 for 'Ad-hoc/ Short Review / Renewal of Credit Facilities' and
DBS.CO.PPD.BC.No.

Credit assessments carried out by a bank shall suitably factor in the possible impact
of calamities on borrowers who may be impacted by such events.
requirements.object: possible impact of calamities on borrowers who may be impacted by such events

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Section 20(1)(b) of the BR Act, 1949, a bank is prohibited from
entering into any commitment for granting any loans or advances to or on behalf
of any of its directors, or any firm in which any of its directors is interested as
partner, manager, employee or guarantor, or any company [not being a
subsidiary of the banking company or a company registered under Section 8 of
the Companies Act, 2013, or a Government company] of which, or the subsidiary
or the holding company of which any of the directors of the bank is a director,
managing agent, manager, employee or guarantor or in which he holds
substantial interest, or any individual in respect of whom any of its directors is a
partner or guarantor.
requirements.object: commitment for granting any loans or advances to or on behalf of any of its directors, or any firm in which any of its director

requirements.full_sentence:
In exercise of the powers conferred by clause (a) of the Explanation under sub-
section 4 of Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, the following
explanations are provided:
(1) The sanction or grant of credit facilities to companies in India by a foreign bank
having branches in India shall be in compliance with the spirit of Section 20 of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: the spirit of Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

conditions:
if a director in the foreign bank's Board abroad
12Deleted with effect from April 01
requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, a foreign bank branch in India shall
not lend to a firm / company in India, if a director in the foreign bank's Board abroad
12Deleted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Risk Management) - Amendment Directions, 2026 dated January 05, 2026.
requirements.object: / company in India

requirements.full_sentence:
While granting loans and advances against shares, statutory provisions
contained in Sections 19(2) and 19(3) of the BR Act, 1949 shall be strictly
observed.

Therefore, a bank shall
not provide loans to companies for buy-back of shares / securities.
requirements.object: for buy-back of shares / securities

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall have the overall responsibility of ensuring that suitable mechanisms
are put in place for implementation of the policy on lending to related parties by the
bank.
requirements.object: of ensuring that suitable mechanisms are put in place for implementation of the policy on lending to related parties b

requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy (hereinafter called the policy) of a bank, as required in terms of the
extant Directions, shall contain specific provisions relating to 'lending to related
parties' in accordance with the provisions of these Directions.
requirements.object: to 'lending to related parties' in accordance with the provisions of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall
prescribe, inter alia, additional safeguards to address the risks emanating from
lending to related parties.
requirements.object: safeguards to address the risks emanating from lending to related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also have specific provisions for lending to 'specified employees'
of the bank and their relatives.
requirements.object: for lending to 'specified employees' of the bank and their relatives
of the whistleblowing mechanism, encourage employees to communicate confidentially and without the risk of reprisal, legitimate concerns

requirements.full_sentence: The policy shall specify aggregate limits for loans towards related parties.
requirements.object: loans towards related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
Within this
aggregate limit, there shall be sub-limits for loans to a single related party and a
group of related parties.
requirements.object: to a single related party and a group of related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
These limits shall be within the extant prudential exposure
limits prescribed by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: prudential exposure limits prescribed by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Provisions of paragraph 14 in Chapter IV shall also apply to grant of loans and
advances to spouse and minor / dependent children of the directors of banks.
requirements.object: of loans and advances to spouse and minor / dependent children of the directors of banks

requirements.full_sentence:
However, banks may grant loan or advance to or on behalf of spouses of their
directors in cases where the spouse has his / her own independent source of
income arising out of his / her employment or profession and the facility so granted
is based on standard procedures and norms for assessing the creditworthiness of
the borrower.
requirements.object: to or on behalf of spouses of their directors in cases where the spouse has his / her own independent source of income arising o

requirements.full_sentence:
Such facility should be extended on commercial terms, conforming
to arms-length principle.
requirements.object: terms, conforming to arms-length principle

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the restrictions placed on loans and advances by a bank to its directors
and the companies in which its directors are interested under Section 20 of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949, a bank shall not have any exposure (including
investments in the equity / debt capital instruments) to its promoters and their
relatives;
requirements.object: investments in the equity / debt capital instruments) to its promoters and their relatives

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, nothing in paragraph 42H above shall apply in cases where a
financial institution, a scheduled commercial bank, a foreign portfolio investor or a
mutual fund holds ten per cent or more of the equity share capital of the bank as
non-strategic investment and without any control of the bank.
requirements.object: a financial institution, a scheduled commercial bank, a foreign portfolio investor or a mutual fund holds ten per cent or more of

requirements.full_sentence:
Loans to related parties, which are not prohibited in terms of provisions of Chapter
IV or this Chapter of these Directions, or which have been permitted under power
exercised by the Reserve Bank under clause (a) of the Explanation under Section
20(4) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, except (i) credit facilities fully secured
by cash or liquid securities and in accordance to prescribed LTV and valuation
norms for such securities and (ii) Interbank loans, shall be subject to a materiality
threshold as per the credit policy, which shall not be higher than the following
ceilings:
Asset Size of the bank (in ₹ crore) Materiality Threshold Ceilings
> 10,00,000 ₹25 crore
≥ 1,00,000 to up to 10,00,000 ₹10 crore
Less than 100,000 ₹5 crore
Asset size based on the last audited balance sheet.
requirements.object: following ceilings: Asset Size of the bank (in ₹ crore) Materiality Threshold Ceilings > 10,00,000 ₹25 crore ≥ 1,00,000 to up to 10

requirements.full_sentence: For loans, materiality threshold shall apply at individual transaction level.
requirements.object: level

requirements.full_sentence:
Materiality thresholds may vary for different categories of loans to related parties as
per the bank's policy.
requirements.object: of loans to related parties as per the bank's policy

requirements.full_sentence:
All loans above the prescribed materiality threshold shall be sanctioned either by the
Board or by the 'Committee on Lending to Related Parties' of the bank.
requirements.object: the Board or by the 'Committee on Lending to Related Parties' of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Directors, KMP, or 'specified employees' shall recuse themselves from deliberations
and decision on loan proposals, or contracts and arrangements, involving
themselves or their related parties.
requirements.object: and decision on loan proposals, or contracts and arrangements, involving themselves or their related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
Such recusal shall also extend to deliberations
and decisions relating to any subsequent material changes to the terms of such
<image>
loans, including one-time settlements, write-offs, waivers, enforcement of security,
implementation of resolution plans, etc.
requirements.object: and decisions relating to any subsequent material changes to the terms of such <image> loans, including one-time settleme

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place a suitable mechanism for maintaining and periodically
updating the list of all the related persons, and the related parties thereof, as well
as the loans sanctioned by the bank to such related persons and related parties.
requirements.object: suitable mechanism for maintaining and periodically updating the list of all the related persons, and the related parties thereof, as w

requirements.full_sentence:
Credit facilities sanctioned to 'specified employees' and their relatives shall be
reported to the Board on an annual basis.
requirements.object: Board on an annual basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Periodic reviews shall be conducted at quarterly or shorter intervals by internal
auditors to check, inter alia, whether guidelines and procedures in relation to loans
to related parties are being adhered to or not.
requirements.object: or shorter intervals by internal auditors to check, inter alia, whether guidelines and procedures in relation to loans to re

requirements.full_sentence:
Any deviation from the policy relating to lending to related parties and reasons
therefor shall be reported to the Audit Committee of the Board.
requirements.object: Audit Committee of the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
Any product, entity or structure formed with the objective of circumventing these
Directions through various means, such as reciprocal lending or quid pro quo
arrangements, and identified as such by the auditors of the bank or by the
supervisory authority and investigating agencies shall always be treated as lending
to related party.
requirements.object: lending to related party

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the provisions of Chapter V on lending to related parties, listed banks
shall continue to comply with the applicable provisions of the Securities and
Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements)
Regulations, 2015, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: the applicable provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regu

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks shall also adhere to instructions placed in terms of Chapter VI- Prudential
Limits on Intra-Group Transactions and Exposure of Reserve Bank of India
<image>
(Commercial Banks - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025 for
transactions with their group entities.
requirements.object: placed in terms of Chapter VI- Prudential Limits on Intra-Group Transactions and Exposure of Reserve Bank of India <image>

requirements.full_sentence:
Any non-compliance with and circumvention of these Directions shall result in
supervisory and enforcement actions as deemed appropriate by the Reserve
Bank.
requirements.object: enforcement actions as deemed appropriate by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
These actions may include imposition of monetary penalty, requirement of
full provisioning, directions to conduct staff accountability exercises, forensic
audits, and restrictions or any other supervisory and enforcement actions as
deemed fit.]
requirements.object: penalty, requirement of full provisioning, directions to conduct staff accountability exercises, forensic audits, and r

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not extend finance for setting up of new units consuming /
producing the Ozone Depleting Substances (ODS).
requirements.object: up of new units consuming / producing the Ozone Depleting Substances (ODS)

requirements.full_sentence:
No financial assistance
shall be extended to small / medium scale units engaged in the manufacture
of the aerosol units using chlorofluorocarbons (CFC) and no refinance would
be extended to any project assisted in this sector.
requirements.object: / medium scale units engaged in the manufacture of the aerosol units using chlorofluorocarbons (CFC) and no refinance would be ex

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall formulate appropriate, well documented, and clearly defined 'Country
Risk Management' policy within the Credit Risk Management policy prescribed in
requirements.object: and clearly defined 'Country Risk Management' policy within the Credit Risk Management policy prescribed in

requirements.full_sentence:
'Country Risk
Management' policy shall address the issues of identifying, measuring, monitoring,
and controlling country exposure risks.
requirements.object: identifying, measuring, monitoring, and controlling country exposure risks

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall specify the responsibility and
accountability of the various levels for the country risk management decisions.
requirements.object: accountability of the various levels for the country risk management decisions

requirements.full_sentence:
The
bank shall also put in place procedures for ensuring that necessary steps are taken
in accordance with the policy.
requirements.object: procedures for ensuring that necessary steps are taken in accordance with the policy

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall be periodically reviewed by the Board
on the basis of the experience gained.
requirements.object: the Board on the basis of the experience gained

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall institute appropriate procedures for dealing with country risk problems.
requirements.object: dealing with country risk problems

requirements.full_sentence:
They shall have in place contingency plans and clear exit strategies, which would be
activated at times of crisis.
requirements.object: plans and clear exit strategies, which would be activated at times of crisis

requirements.full_sentence:
Appropriate systems and procedures shall be laid down
with the approval of the Board to handle situations involving significant changes in
conditions in any country.
requirements.object: the approval of the Board to handle situations involving significant changes in conditions in any country

requirements.full_sentence:
The Country Risk Management policy shall stipulate rigorous application of the 'Know
Your Customer' (KYC) principle in international activities which shall not be
compromised by availability of collateral or shortening of maturities.
requirements.object: of collateral or shortening of maturities

requirements.full_sentence:
Country risk
element shall be explicitly recognised while assessing the counter-party risk.
requirements.object: assessing the counter-party risk

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall reckon both funded and non-funded exposures from their domestic as
well as foreign branches while identifying, measuring, monitoring, and controlling
country risks.
requirements.object: non-funded exposures from their domestic as well as foreign branches while identifying, measuring, monitoring, and controlling

requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of foreign banks operating in India, the scope shall be
confined to their branches in India.
requirements.object: branches in India

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall refer to Reserve Bank of India
<image>
(Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025 for permissible Non-Fund
based credit facilities.
requirements.object: of India <image> (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025 for permissible Non-Fund based credit facilities

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall take into account indirect country risk.
requirements.object: country risk

requirements.full_sentence:
For example, exposures to a
domestic commercial borrower with a large economic dependence on a certain
country shall be considered as subject to indirect country risk.
requirements.object: to indirect country risk

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place appropriate systems to move over to internal assessment of
country risk and shall evolve sound systems for measuring and monitoring country
risk.
requirements.object: systems to move over to internal assessment of country risk and shall evolve sound systems for measuring and monitoring count

requirements.full_sentence:
The system shall be able to identify the full dimensions of country risk as well as
incorporating features that acknowledge the links between credit and market risk.
requirements.object: the full dimensions of country risk as well as incorporating features that acknowledge the links between credit and market risk

requirements.full_sentence:
A
bank shall use a variety of internal and external sources as a means to measure
country risk.
requirements.object: internal and external sources as a means to measure country risk

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not rely solely on rating agencies or other external sources
as their only country risk-monitoring tool and shall also incorporate information from
the relevant country managers of their foreign branches into their country risk
assessments.
requirements.object: agencies or other external sources as their only country risk-monitoring tool and shall also incorporate information from the re

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the rating accorded by a bank to any country shall not be
<image>
better than the rating of that country by an international rating agency.
requirements.object: the rating of that country by an international rating agency

conditions:
where a bank's net funded exposure is one per cent or
more of its total assets
requirements.full_sentence:
For the present,
only in respect of the country, where a bank's net funded exposure is one per cent or
more of its total assets, the bank is required to undertake internal assessment of
country risk rating.
requirements.object: of country risk rating

requirements.full_sentence:
The frequency of periodic reviews of country risk ratings shall be at least once a year
with a provision to review the rating of specific country, based on any major events in
that country, where bank exposure is high, even before the next periodical review of
the ratings is due.
requirements.object: a year with a provision to review the rating of specific country, based on any major events in that country

requirements.full_sentence:
Till such time, as banks move over to internal rating system, a bank may use the
seven-category classification followed by Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of
India Ltd.
requirements.object: followed by Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of India Ltd

requirements.full_sentence:
ECGC shall provide to a bank, on request, quarterly updates of their
country classifications and shall also inform all banks in case of any sudden major
changes in country classification in the interim period.
requirements.object: on request, quarterly updates of their country classifications and shall also inform all banks in case of any sudden major changes

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall monitor their country exposures on a real time basis.
requirements.object: on a real time basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Management of country risk shall incorporate stress testing as one method to monitor
actual and potential risks.
requirements.object: one method to monitor actual and potential risks

requirements.full_sentence:
Stress testing shall include an assessment of the impact of
alternative outcomes to important underlying assumptions.
requirements.object: the impact of alternative outcomes to important underlying assumptions

requirements.full_sentence: Boards shall review the country risk exposures at quarterly intervals.
requirements.object: exposures at quarterly intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
The review shall
include progress in establishing internal country rating systems, compliance with the
regulatory and the internal limits, results of stress tests and the exit options available
to the banks in respect of countries belonging to 'high risk & above' categories.
requirements.object: internal country rating systems, compliance with the regulatory and the internal limits, results of stress tests and

requirements.full_sentence:
In
case, any significant deterioration takes place in respect of any particular country risk
or overall exposure, banks shall report to the Board such developments in its next
meeting, without waiting for the quarterly review by the Board.
requirements.object: such developments in its next meeting, without waiting for the quarterly review by the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
Country risk management processes employed by a bank shall require adequate
<image>
internal controls that include audits or other appropriate oversight mechanisms to
ensure the integrity of the information used by senior officials in overseeing
compliance with policies and limits.
requirements.object: controls that include audits or other appropriate oversight mechanisms to ensure the integrity of the information us

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall assess and monitor the Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure
(UCFE) of entities and maintain adequate provisioning / capital for the same, as
per the instructions provided in this Chapter.
requirements.object: Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UCFE) of entities and maintain adequate provisioning / capital for the same, as per the ins

requirements.full_sentence:
Computation of UFCE
(1) A bank shall ascertain the Foreign Currency Exposure (FCE) of all entities at
least on an annual basis.
requirements.object: Exposure (FCE) of all entities at least on an annual basis

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compute the FCE following the relevant
accounting standard applicable for the entity.
requirements.object: the relevant accounting standard applicable for the entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation:
Note: For arriving at the foreign currency exposure of entities, their exposure from
all sources including foreign currency borrowings and External Commercial
Borrowings shall be taken into account.

(2) A bank shall assess the Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) of
entities with FCE by obtaining information on UFCE from the concerned entity.
requirements.object: Currency Exposure (UFCE) of entities with FCE by obtaining information on UFCE from the concerned entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the information on UFCE shall be obtained from entities on a
quarterly basis based on statutory audit, internal audit, or self-declaration by the
concerned entity.
requirements.object: on a quarterly basis based on statutory audit, internal audit, or self-declaration by the concerned entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that UFCE information shall be audited and certified by the
statutory auditors of the entity, at least on an annual basis.
requirements.object: by the statutory auditors of the entity, at least on an annual basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: A bank shall use the data published by FEDAI on largest annual
volatility of USD-INR rate over a period of last 10 years and the same shall be
used for computation of potential loss by multiplying it with UFCE.
requirements.object: by FEDAI on largest annual volatility of USD-INR rate over a period of last 10 years and the same shall be used for computation

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: The UFCE in currencies other than USD shall be converted into USD using
the current market rates for determining the potential loss from UFCE.
requirements.object: using the current market rates for determining the potential loss from UFCE

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall determine the susceptibility of the entity to adverse exchange rate
movements by computing the ratio of the potential loss to entity from UFCE and
the entity's EBID over the last four quarters as per the latest quarterly results
certified by the statutory auditors.
requirements.object: the entity to adverse exchange rate movements by computing the ratio of the potential loss to entity from UFCE and the

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: (i) In cases where a bank is not in a position to obtain information on UFCE
or EBID from listed entities for the latest quarter due to restrictions on disclosure
of such information prior to finalisation of accounts, the bank shall have the option
to use data pertaining to the immediately preceding last four quarters for
computing capital and provisioning requirements.
requirements.object: use data pertaining to the immediately preceding last four quarters for computing capital and provisioning requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
Table 1: Incremental Provisioning and Capital Requirements for UFCE
Incremental Provisioning Requirement (basis
Potential Loss / EBID
Incremental Capital
(per cent)
Requirement
points)
Upto 15 per cent 0 0 More than 15 per cent and upto 30 per cent 20bps 0
More than 30 per cent and upto 50 per cent 40bps 0
More than 50 percent and upto 75 per cent 60bps 0
More than 75 per cent 80bps 25 per centage point increase in the risk
weight
Explanation:
Note: The incremental provisioning for UFCE shall be based on the total exposure
amount which is used for computing standard asset provisioning and the
incremental capital requirements for UFCE shall be based on the total exposure
amount which is used for computing credit risk capital requirements.
requirements.object: total exposure amount which is used for computing standard asset provisioning and the incremental capital requirements for UFCE shall

requirements.full_sentence: (4) A bank shall calculate the incremental provisioning and capital requirements
requirements.object: and capital requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
Systems and Controls
(1) A bank shall incorporate the risk of UFCE of entities in their internal credit rating
system and credit risk management policies and procedures.
requirements.object: UFCE of entities in their internal credit rating system and credit risk management policies and procedures

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall stipulate internal limits for UFCE within the overall Board approved
risk policy of the bank.
requirements.object: UFCE within the overall Board approved risk policy of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Consortium Lending
In the case of consortium / Multiple Banking Arrangements (MBAs), the
consortium leader / bank having the largest exposure shall have the lead role in
monitoring the unhedged foreign exchange exposure of entities.
requirements.object: in monitoring the unhedged foreign exchange exposure of entities

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: A banks shall put in place a system for information sharing and
dissemination in terms of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Transfer
and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: system for information sharing and dissemination in terms of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Transfer and Distribution of Cre

requirements.full_sentence:
Exemption / Relaxation
A bank shall have the option to exclude the following exposures from the
calculation of UFCE:
(1) Exposures to entities classified as sovereign, banks, and individuals;
requirements.object: exclude the following exposures from the calculation of UFCE: (1) Exposures to entities classified as sovereign, banks, and individ

requirements.full_sentence:
Overseas Branches / Subsidiaries
(1) The provisions of this Chapter shall also be applicable to overseas branches
/ subsidiaries of a bank subject to the following:
Note: A bank shall compute the largest annual volatility over a period of last 10
years in the following manner: First, daily changes in the foreign exchange rates
shall be computed as a log return of today's rate over the previous day's rate.
requirements.object: overseas branches / subsidiaries of a bank subject to the following: Note: A bank shall compute the largest annual volatility ov

requirements.full_sentence:
Second, daily volatility shall be computed as standard deviation of these returns
over a period of one year (250 observations).
requirements.object: deviation of these returns over a period of one year (250 observations)

requirements.full_sentence:
Third, this daily volatility shall be
annualised by multiplying it by square root of 250.
requirements.object: it by square root of 250

requirements.full_sentence:
This computation shall be
performed on a daily basis for all the days in the last 10 years.
requirements.object: daily basis for all the days in the last 10 years

requirements.full_sentence:
The largest annual
volatility thus computed shall be used for the computation of the potential loss by
multiplying it with the UFCE.
requirements.object: computation of the potential loss by multiplying it with the UFCE

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) With respect to the exposure to entities incorporated outside India, information
on UFCE shall be obtained from such entities on a quarterly basis based on
internal audit or self-declaration and the requirement of certificate from statutory
auditors on annual basis, as provided in paragraph 57(2), may not be insisted
upon.
requirements.object: entities on a quarterly basis based on internal audit or self-declaration and the requirement of certificate from statutory audi

conditions:
where a bank is not able to obtain information on UFCE from
concerned entities
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where a bank is not able to obtain information on UFCE from
concerned entities, the treatment provided in paragraph 58(6) shall apply.

(ii) A bank shall compute the potential loss due to UFCE by replacing INR with
the domestic currency of that jurisdiction and USD with the foreign currency (i.e.,
currency other than domestic currency of that jurisdiction) in which the entity has
maximum exposure in paragraph 58(1).
requirements.object: due to UFCE by replacing INR with the domestic currency of that jurisdiction and USD with the foreign currency (i

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that non-individual borrowers with aggregate exposure of ₹5
crore and above from banks (Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding Regional
Rural Banks), Local Area Banks, Small Finance Banks, and Primary (Urban) Co-
operative Banks) and Financial Institutions (All India Financial Institutions and
NBFCs (including HFCs)) obtain LEI codes.
requirements.object: with aggregate exposure of ₹5 crore and above from banks (Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding Regional Rural Ba

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: 'Exposure' for this purpose shall include all fund based and non-fund
based (credit as well as investment) exposure of banks / FIs to the borrower.
requirements.object: and non-fund based (credit as well as investment) exposure of banks / FIs to the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
Aggregate sanctioned limit or outstanding balance, whichever is higher, shall be
reckoned for the purpose.
requirements.object: purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
Lenders shall ascertain the position of aggregate
exposure based on information available either with them, or Central Repository of
Information on Large Credits (CRILC) database or declaration obtained from the
borrower.
requirements.object: aggregate exposure based on information available either with them, or Central Repository of Information on Large Credits (C

requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers who fail to obtain LEI codes from an authorised Local Operating Unit (LOU)
shall not be sanctioned any new exposure nor shall they be granted renewal /
enhancement of any existing exposure.
requirements.object: exposure nor shall they be granted renewal / enhancement of any existing exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
However, Departments / Agencies of Central
and State Governments (not Public Sector Undertakings registered under Companies
Act or established as Corporation under the relevant statute) shall be exempted from
this provision.
requirements.object: provision

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall encourage large borrowers to obtain LEI for their parent entity as well as
all subsidiaries and associates.
requirements.object: obtain LEI for their parent entity as well as all subsidiaries and associates

requirements.full_sentence:
In India, LEI code may be obtained from Legal Entity Identifier India Ltd
(LEIIL), a subsidiary of the Clearing Corporation of India Limited (CCIL), which has
been recognised by the Reserve Bank as issuer of LEI under the Payment and
Settlement Systems Act, 2007 and is accredited by the GLEIF as the Local Operating
Unit (LOU) in India for issuance and management of LEI.
requirements.object: Entity Identifier India Ltd (LEIIL), a subsidiary of the Clearing Corporation of India Limited (CCIL), which has been recognise

requirements.full_sentence:
The rules, procedure and
documentation requirements may be ascertained from LEIIL.

After obtaining LEI code, a bank shall also ensure that borrowers renew the codes as
per GLEIF guidelines.
requirements.object: renew the codes as per GLEIF guidelines

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall be guided by the following aspects while formulating a policy on valuation
of properties and appointment of valuers.
requirements.object: following aspects while formulating a policy on valuation of properties and appointment of valuers

requirements.full_sentence:
Policy for valuation of properties
(1) A bank shall have a Board approved policy in place for valuation of properties
including collaterals accepted for their exposures.
requirements.object: policy in place for valuation of properties including collaterals accepted for their exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The valuation shall be done by professionally qualified independent valuers
i.e.
requirements.object: qualified independent valuers i

requirements.full_sentence: the valuer shall not have a direct or indirect interest.
requirements.object: indirect interest

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall obtain minimum two Independent valuation reports for properties
valued at ₹50 crore or above.
requirements.object: valuation reports for properties valued at ₹50 crore or above

requirements.full_sentence:
Revaluation of bank's own properties
In addition to the above, banks may keep the following aspects in view while
formulating policy for revaluation of their own properties:
(1) As a bank is permitted to include revaluation reserves as part of Capital in terms
of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital
Adequacy) Directions, 2025, it is necessary that revaluation reserves represent
true appreciation in the market value of the properties and banks have in place a
comprehensive policy for revaluation of fixed assets owned by them.
requirements.object: in view while formulating policy for revaluation of their own properties: (1) As a bank is permitted to include revaluation

requirements.full_sentence:
Such a policy
shall inter alia cover procedure for identification of assets for revaluation,
maintenance of separate set of records for such assets, the frequency of
revaluation, depreciation policy for such assets, policy for sale of such revalued
assets etc.
requirements.object: for identification of assets for revaluation, maintenance of separate set of records for such assets, the frequency of reva

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also cover the disclosure required to be made in the
'Notes on Account' regarding the details of revaluation such as the original cost of
the fixed assets subject to revaluation and accounting treatment for appreciation /
depreciation etc.
requirements.object: required to be made in the 'Notes on Account' regarding the details of revaluation such as the original cost of the fixed as

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) As the revaluation shall reflect the change in the fair value of the fixed asset, the
frequency of revaluation shall be determined based on the observed volatility in
the prices of the assets in the past.
requirements.object: the fair value of the fixed asset, the frequency of revaluation shall be determined based on the observed volatility in the pric

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, any change in the method of
<image>
depreciation shall reflect the change in the expected pattern of consumption of the
future economic benefits of the assets.
requirements.object: the expected pattern of consumption of the future economic benefits of the assets

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall adhere to these principles
meticulously while changing the frequency of revaluation / method of depreciation
for a particular class of asset and shall make proper disclosures in this regard.
requirements.object: meticulously while changing the frequency of revaluation / method of depreciation for a particular class of asset and shall

requirements.full_sentence:
Policy for Empanelment of Independent valuers
(1) A bank shall have a procedure for empanelment of professional valuers and
maintain a register / record of 'approved list of valuers'.
requirements.object: empanelment of professional valuers and maintain a register / record of 'approved list of valuers'

requirements.full_sentence: (2) A bank may prescribe a minimum qualification for empanelment of valuers.
requirements.object: for empanelment of valuers

requirements.full_sentence: Different qualifications may be prescribed for different classes of assets (e.g.
requirements.object: classes of assets (e

requirements.full_sentence:
While prescribing the
qualification, the bank shall take into consideration the qualifications prescribed
under Section 34AB (Rule 8A) of the Wealth Tax Act, 1957.
requirements.object: qualifications prescribed under Section 34AB (Rule 8A) of the Wealth Tax Act, 1957

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall also be guided by the relevant Accounting Standard issued by the
Institute of Chartered Accountants of India.
requirements.object: the relevant Accounting Standard issued by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India

requirements.full_sentence:
The records
maintained by the Central Registry shall be available for search by any lender or any
other person desirous of dealing with the property.
requirements.object: by any lender or any other person desirous of dealing with the property

requirements.full_sentence:
It may be noted that under the provisions of Section 23 of the SARFAESI
Act, every transaction of security interest is required to be filed with the Registry.
requirements.object: the provisions of Section 23 of the SARFAESI Act, every transaction of security interest is required to be filed with the Registry

requirements.full_sentence:
Current Accounts, Cash Credit Accounts (CC), and Overdraft Accounts (OD) may
all be used as transaction accounts by the customers, which raises concerns relating
to credit monitoring by the lenders.
requirements.object: transaction accounts by the customers, which raises concerns relating to credit monitoring by the lenders

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation (1): 'Banking System' for the purpose of this Chapter shall include
Commercial Banks (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks, and Regional
Rural Banks, but excluding Payments Banks), Urban Co-operative Banks and Rural
Co-operative Banks (State Co-operative Banks and Central Co-operative Banks).
requirements.object: Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks, and Regional Rural Banks, but excluding Payments Banks), Urban Co-operative

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation (2): 'Exposure' for the purpose of this Chapter means the sum of all
sanctioned fund-based credit facilities and non-fund-based facilities availed by the
borrower from the banking system.
requirements.object: sanctioned fund-based credit facilities and non-fund-based facilities availed by the borrower from the banking system

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: 'Collection Account' for the purpose of this Chapter means a current
account or OD account used primarily for receipts of cash inflows of the account
holder.
requirements.object: OD account used primarily for receipts of cash inflows of the account holder

requirements.full_sentence:
Restricted payments / cash outflows from such account shall be subject to
the conditions outlined in paragraph 91F of these Directions.
requirements.object: conditions outlined in paragraph 91F of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may provide such cash credit facilities as per the needs of the
customer, without any restriction under this Chapter.
requirements.object: facilities as per the needs of the customer, without any restriction under this Chapter

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may maintain current account or OD account without any restriction in case
of customers where the aggregate exposure of the banking system to the customer
is less than ₹10 crore.
requirements.object: OD account without any restriction in case of customers where the aggregate exposure of the banking system to the customer

requirements.full_sentence:
Funds credited into a collection account shall be remitted within two working days
of receipt of such funds to a CC account, current account, or OD account maintained
with any bank in the banking system and designated by the borrower for this purpose
(hereinafter referred to as 'designated account' in this Chapter).
requirements.object: working days of receipt of such funds to a CC account, current account, or OD account maintained with any bank in the banking s

requirements.full_sentence:
Any disbursement of
overdraft limit from an OD account, which is in the nature of a collection account, shall
be through the designated account only.
requirements.object: account only

conditions: Provided that statutory dues such as taxes
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that statutory dues such as taxes, and dues, if any, to the bank maintaining
the collection account may be debited before remitting the funds.
requirements.object: taxes, and dues

requirements.full_sentence:
The restrictions placed in terms of paragraph 91D(1) of these Directions shall not
be applicable to the accounts mentioned below:
(1) Accounts opened as per the provisions of Foreign Exchange Management Act,
1999 (FEMA) and notifications issued thereunder, including accounts mandated
for ensuring compliance under the FEMA framework.
requirements.object: accounts mentioned below: (1) Accounts opened as per the provisions of Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (FEMA) and notificat

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that banks operating the above-mentioned exempted accounts shall
ensure that transactions in such accounts are used only for the permitted /
requirements.object: such accounts are used only for the permitted /

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of ensuring ongoing compliance with this Chapter, all banks shall
monitor accounts maintained with them on a regular basis, and in any case at least
once every half-year.
requirements.object: them on a regular basis, and in any case at least once every half-year

requirements.full_sentence:
then the bank shall notify the customer(s) concerned promptly, and in any case within
one month from the date of observing such ineligibility, that the account must either
be converted to a collection account or closed.
requirements.object: promptly, and in any case within one month from the date of observing such ineligibility, that the account must eithe

requirements.full_sentence:
The conversion or closure process, as
the case may be, shall be completed within three months of observing such
ineligibility.
requirements.object: months of observing such ineligibility

requirements.full_sentence:
Accounts opened in terms of these Directions shall be appropriately flagged in the
bank's core banking solution (CBS) to ensure clear identification and to facilitate
effective monitoring.
requirements.object: the bank's core banking solution (CBS) to ensure clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks maintaining multiple accounts for a borrower shall ensure
that such accounts and transactions and cashflows therein are monitored at the
borrower level as also at the account level.
requirements.object: and transactions and cashflows therein are monitored at the borrower level as also at the account level

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that an accountholder utilise their account solely for
transactions related to their authorised business or activities.
requirements.object: utilise their account solely for transactions related to their authorised business or activities

requirements.full_sentence:
These accounts shall
not, under any circumstances, be used as pass-through channels for facilitating third-
party transactions.
requirements.object: be used as pass-through channels for facilitating third- party transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that entities expressly licensed or authorised by a financial sector regulator
to facilitate third-party transactions may continue to do so.
requirements.object: authorised by a financial sector regulator to facilitate third-party transactions may continue to do so

requirements.full_sentence:
However, such activities
shall strictly be limited to the specific transactions they are authorised to do and shall
not extend beyond that scope.
requirements.object: the specific transactions they are authorised to do and shall not extend beyond that scope

requirements.full_sentence:
Any account that has been permitted to carry out such
third-party transactions shall be appropriately flagged in the bank's CBS to ensure
clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring.
requirements.object: the bank's CBS to ensure clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that an accountholder, who is not licensed or authorised by
the Reserve Bank to accept deposits or to provide payment services, do not engage
in such activities through accounts maintained with them.
requirements.object: who is not licensed or authorised by the Reserve Bank to accept deposits or to provide payment services, do not engage i

requirements.full_sentence:
Robust monitoring systems shall be implemented to detect the above prohibited
usage, including mechanisms to flag accounts exhibiting unusually high transaction
<image>
volumes, frequent pass-through activities, or inconsistencies between the
accountholder's stated line of business and transactions carried out through the
account.
requirements.object: the above prohibited usage, including mechanisms to flag accounts exhibiting unusually high transaction <image> volumes, freq

requirements.full_sentence:
Term loans sanctioned by the bank shall preferably be remitted directly to the
intended beneficiary's account(s) or for the specified end-use, where such beneficiary
is identifiable, rather than routing the funds through the borrower's account.]
<image>
requirements.object: to the intended beneficiary's account(s) or for the specified end-use

requirements.full_sentence:
With a view to enhance credit discipline among the larger borrowers enjoying
working capital facility from the banking system, delivery of bank credit for such
borrowers shall be as under:
(1) Minimum level of 'loan component'
In respect of borrowers having aggregate fund based working capital limit (WCL) of
₹150 crore and above from the banking system (excluding intra-day credit facilities),
the outstanding 'loan component' (Working Capital Loan) must be equal to at least
60 per cent of the sanctioned fund based working capital limit, including ad hoc limits
and TODs.
requirements.object: Minimum level of 'loan component' In respect of borrowers having aggregate fund based working capital limit (WCL) of ₹150 crore and a

requirements.full_sentence:
Hence, for such borrowers, drawings up to 60 per cent of the total fund
based working capital limits shall only be allowed from the 'loan component'.
requirements.object: the 'loan component'

requirements.full_sentence:
Drawings in excess of the minimum 'loan component' threshold may be allowed in
the form of cash credit / overdraft facility.
requirements.object: form of cash credit / overdraft facility

requirements.full_sentence:
The bifurcation of the working capital limit
into loan and cash credit / overdraft components shall be effected after excluding the
export credit limits (pre-shipment and post-shipment) and bills limit for inland sales
from the working capital limit.
requirements.object: the export credit limits (pre-shipment and post-shipment) and bills limit for inland sales from the working capital limit

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment by the bank in the commercial papers issued
by the borrower shall form part of the loan component, provided the investment is
sanctioned as part of the working capital limit.
requirements.object: loan component

requirements.full_sentence:
The above instructions on minimum loan component shall not be applicable on the
following:
requirements.object: following:

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Sharing of Working Capital Finance
Bifurcation of working capital facility into loan component and cash credit / overdraft
component shall be maintained at individual bank level in all cases, including
consortium lending.
requirements.object: bank level in all cases, including consortium lending

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(3) Amount and tenor of the loan
The amount and tenor of the loan component may be fixed by banks in consultation
with the borrowers, subject to the tenor being not less than seven days.
requirements.object: in consultation with the borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks may
decide to split the loan component into WCLs with different maturity periods as per
the needs of the borrowers.
requirements.object: loan component into WCLs with different maturity periods as per the needs of the borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may consider rollover of the WCLs at the
request of the borrower, subject to compliance with the directions ibid.
requirements.object: WCLs at the request of the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) For credit conversion factor for undrawn portion of working capital limits
sanctioned to the aforesaid large borrowers, a bank shall refer to Reserve
Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy)
Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions and guidelines
repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the Reserve Bank may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank shall be final and binding.

These losses may reduce their
capacity to service the loans taken from the banking system and increase their
probability of default thereby affecting the health of the banking system.

requirements.full_sentence:
The aim of the framework was that the banks shall price the risk from Unhedged
Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) as credit risk premium which may nudge entities
to hedge their foreign currency exposures in the market.
requirements.object: Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) as credit risk premium which may nudge entities to hedge their foreign currency exposure
